title: I One-Shotted An Entire App With Claude Fable 5 (Full Breakdown)
summary: Jordan Urbs demonstrates building a video editing app in one hour using Claude Fable 5 with only screenshots as input, no PRD or specs. He cautions that Fable 5 will be discontinued from Claude plans on June 22 and consumes token limits 2x faster than Opus, advocating for local-first deployment via Venice API and questioning whether developers should adopt the model given its short availability window.
writeup: Jordan Urbs builds a video editing app in roughly one hour using Claude Fable 5, supplying only screenshots as input with no PRD or written specs. The video matters because it tests whether a single prompt-style workflow can produce a working app, and because Fable 5 carries practical constraints: it consumes token limits twice as fast as Opus and is scheduled for discontinuation from Claude plans on June 22. Key takeaways include the screenshot-driven build approach, the case for local-first deployment via the Venice API, and the open question of whether adopting a model with a short availability window is worthwhile.
